text: Minutes of the meeting of Alconbury Weston Parish Council
Councillors present: Mrs. J. Baker, Mr. M. Bryan, Mr. S. Lomas, Dr. C. McGregor & Mr. L. York.
Also present: District Cllr. K. Baker & Mrs. D. Benham (Parish Clerk).
1. Apologies for absence: County Cllr. P. Ashcroft (Attending another meeting), County Cllr. P.
Brown (Personal), Cllr. R. Coulson (Personal) & Cllr. H. Mobius (Work commitment).
3. Public participation: There were no parishioners present.
4. To approve the minutes of the last meeting held on 1st September 2014 : The minutes of the
meeting held on 1st September 2014 were approved and signed by the Chairman.
– a) Alconbury Weald The next liaison meeting will be held on 14th October 2014.
– b) Parish Council website The new website is due to go live on 21st October 2014.
– c) Cambridgeshire Future Transport No further news.
– d) Incident on Buckworth Road involving a wide load County Cllr. Brown is dealing with this
e) Village Green - Cllr. Lomas obtained a quotation from Stuart Fry of £100 to reseed and apply
weed treatment to an area of the village green which councillors approved by email. Cllr. McGregor
proposed retrospective approval of the action taken by the Clerk and Cllr. Lomas in accepting the
quote. Seconded by Cllr. Baker and unanimously agreed. The Clerk will remind residents that
parking is not allowed on the village green in the next Parish Pump article.
Cllr. McGregor had nothing to report.
Cllr. Lomas advised that there were no issues to report, but he had trimmed back the suckers on the
– 1401557LBC Proposed triple garage. 72 Hamerton Road, Alconbury Weston. Cllr. Lomas
proposed that Alconbury Weston Parish Council recommend approval. Seconded by Cllr.
b) Update on previously discussed applications.
– 1401265FUL Single storey mono pitch roof extension and entrance porch. 43 Highfield Avenue,
– 1401318FUL Erection of stable building for personal use only. Longacres, High Street,
– 1401344FUL Replacement of permitted mobile home (log cabin) with existing building to include
reduced ridge height and additional timber cladding (part retrospective) for mixed use building as
office, staff/mess room, storage and dwelling. OS9351 Buckworth Road, Alconbury Weston.
Application no longer available to view on HDC’s website.
1300666FUL, Change of use from vacant land to provide six permanent pitches for gypsies and
travellers. Former Megatron site, Ermine Street. Appeal in progress.
Cllr. Baker proposed retrospective approval of payment of items (1) to (5) and approval of payment
of item (6). Seconded by Cllr. York and unanimously agreed.
(1) SR Howell & Co. - £60.00, payroll services to
(2) Mrs. D. Benham - £193.69, wages & expenses of Parish Clerk for September. Cq. No.
(4) PKF Littlejohn LLP - £120.00, external audit fee. Cq. No. 100990
(5) Business Services at CAS - £397.96, Parish Council insurance. Cq. No. 100991
Councillors agreed that they did not wish to renew the annual membership of Cambridgeshire
b) Current position - A copy of the receipts and payments and bank reconciliation was handed to
– c) Result of the external audit The Clerk advised that the external audit had been completed on
28th August with no issues raised and no matters to be brought to the attention of the Parish Council.
A notice advertising the completion of the audit and a copy of the Annual Return had been placed
on the notice boards on 2nd September 2014.
The July and August crime reports both show 2 recorded crimes in Alconbury Weston near to the
Cambridgeshire Children’s a) Centre Stakeholder briefing.
b) Complaint regarding overgrown pump station on High Street passed to Anglian Water to deal.
– c) Invitation for a Parish Councillor to sit on the Woolley Hill Wind Farm Trust panel Cllr. Lomas
volunteered. Notices have also been put up advertising the two spaces for residents of surrounding
– e) Complaint regarding overflowing dog waste bin on Vinegar Hill passed to HDC to deal.
f) Preferred bidder selected to improve Older People’s healthcare and Adult Community Services.
g) Copy of outline planning permission for Alconbury Weald.
h) The Clerk attended a free workshop held by Hunts Forum on Big Lottery Awards for All and the
i) Information on the relaunch of the Quality Parish Council Scheme.
j) Alconbury Weald drop-in sessions. Posters to follow.
Cllr. Lomas attended the Woolley Hill Wind Farm liaison meeting on 23th September and will
attend the first panel meeting on 24th November. District Cllr. Baker advised that he was in contact
with the enforcement officer regarding activity at Buckworth Lodge. Clerk to report a dip in the
carriageway outside The Spinney on Hamerton Road.
Monday 24th November 2014 starting at 7.30pm at Alconbury Memorial Hall.
